name: Lam Research Corporation
window: FQ3 FY26 print → intra-quarter · Apr 22 → Jun 22, 2026

events.src: Lam Research · Q3 FY26 earnings call
events.text: FQ3 FY26 (Mar): revenue $5.84B (+9% q/q, +24% y/y), above the midpoint — 3rd straight quarter of record revenue; GAAP GM 49.9%, operating margin 35%, record diluted EPS $1.47. DRAM hit an all-time high of 27% of systems (+45% y/y); advanced packaging guided to exceed +50% in CY2026; CSBG $2.1B (record). China 34%, Korea and Taiwan 23% each. FQ4 guide: revenue $6.6B ±$400M, GM 50.5%, EPS $1.65 ±$0.15. CY26 WFE raised to ~$140B (from $135B); a ~$40B NAND conversion wave with the majority of spend before the end of CY27. China guided down in the June quarter.

OW all three large caps — "overweight... KLA, Lam, and Applied Materials." CY26 favors depo/etch (LRCX/AMAT), but CY27 rotates to the new-node leaders KLAC/ASML as sub-2nm takes off — a relative forward-revisions headwind vs KLA. NAND greenfield still absent (first one only in CY28). The buy-side bar on WFE is "very, very high" — AMAT sold off on the guide-up because expectations were already there.

"I like Semi Caps, AMAT, LAM. I've been pushing those now for a while" — LRCX+AMAT "the two stocks you want to own." Reads Lam's capacity actions as confirmation of a rising WFE ceiling: a 2nd Malaysia fab + discussion of a 3rd site = signal of WFE heading toward $250B CY28. UBS Buy, PT $200.

MS upgraded LRCX/LAM to Overweight ("memory mismatch unresolved, LAM up to OW, AMAT down to EW"), reversing the prior UW — the fundamental skeptic turned constructive on Lam, placing it above AMAT (cut to EW).
Semis Spring Series: OW LAM among the top semicap picks; flags TSMC with "a lot of urgency... to pull tool orders ahead of previously discussed schedules" — LAM a named beneficiary of the pull-ins.
"WFE Update: Early Innings" — constructive read-through on the WFE cycle; LRCX the most levered to memory capex of the trio.

Shane prefers LAM + KLA over AMAT; LAM seen taking "another strong market-share gain" next year, levered to NAND + an Intel CAPEX recovery (vs AMAT's DRAM WFE deceleration to mid-to-high-20s% in 2027). MS WFE $149B/$191B/+mid-teens for 2026/27/28.

events.src: Morgan Stanley · Tech Talk podcast (Joe Moore & Shane Brett, Ep. 310)
events.text: Post-Asia-trip checks; chapter on "preference among AMAT, LRCX & KLAC." Anchors the MS rating — LAM Overweight (upgrade 2026-05-18), above AMAT (cut to EW); LAM the preferred depo/etch name alongside KLA.

events.src: AlphaSense Deep Research · LRCX primer (Bernstein/Mizuho/expert calls)
events.text: FQ3 FY26 non-GAAP GM 49.9% (vs 49.0% guide); June guide rev $6.60B / GM 50.5% / EPS $1.65 well ahead of the Street ($6.05B / 49.2% / $1.45 per Bernstein). SAM economics: the GAA transition ~doubles Lam's SAM/wafer vs 5nm (~$1B incremental per 100k wfr/mo) + ~$1B from backside-power; HBM ~$450M SAM per 100k wfr/mo. China: the BIS 50% affiliates rule ≈ ~$600M revenue headwind in CY26. Mizuho valuation: LRCX CY27E P/E 36.6x / EV/FCF 40.9x — Outperform PT $380 (bull $500 / bear $305); Bernstein Outperform PT $325.

cells: Acara installed base doubled; GAA apps ~2x
First key foundry/logic dielectric-etch wins; Kiyo ramp win
"Moving faster than expected" on upgrades; greenfield ~2027/28
$40B conversion; majority of spend pulled before end-CY27
DRAM 14% of systems; HBM adoption a driver
Record DRAM 27%, +45% y/y; Stryker ALD; 1c SAM +20%
34%; flattish y/y; guided down in the June quarter
Expansion cited as an etch/depo driver
Guided to exceed 50% growth in CY2026
